name: Senomorphics field change check for June 7, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Senomorphics since the 2026-06-01 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran a field change check from the June 1, 2026 senomorphics baseline publication through June 7, 2026. PubMed searches found one material post-baseline primary source: PMID 42251040, a Nature Communications mianserin/Ca2+ homeostasis study reporting lower senescence in human aging/progeria-derived cells plus improved healthspan and lifespan in progeroid and naturally aged mice. That source supports a staged public update but does not change the human_biomarker_signal stage. ClinicalTrials.gov checks refreshed the two existing human registry-linked records: NCT03103893 remains completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section while the peer-reviewed topical rapamycin result is already in the evidence layer, and NCT03107884 remains active, not recruiting, with no posted registry results while the published metformin bed-rest substudy remains in the evidence layer. Close GLP-1, dasatinib/quercetin delivery, rapalog, JAK, and metformin hits were recorded as context, no-results, or wrong-scope items.

materiality_decision.rationale: PMID 42251040 is a post-baseline primary senomorphics source with animal lifespan/healthspan and human-cell senescence claims, so it should be reviewed for the public evidence map. It does not supply treated-human evidence, so the stage, momentum, confidence, and LEV-relevant boundary stay unchanged. The registry checks are factual study-record refreshes, not evidence upgrades.

search_log.summary: The audit found two registry-linked senomorphics interventional studies and two stale registry checks: NCT03103893 for topical rapamycin human skin aging and NCT03107884 for metformin disuse/re-ambulation in older adults. Both local records already linked peer-reviewed result sources.

NCT03103893 remains completed, last update posted 2021-09-22, with 2017-11-30 actual primary and study completion, 36 actual enrollment, and no registry results section. NCT03107884 remains active, not recruiting, last update posted 2025-12-05, with 2026-11-01 estimated primary and study completion, 64 actual enrollment, and no registry results section.

search_log.query: senescence OR SASP OR senescence-associated secretory phenotype OR senomorphic
search_log.summary: Returned mostly disease or wrong-scope registries. NCT07395960 is a recruiting metformin MCI/overweight older-adult study with no results and no inspected senescence/SASP endpoint, so it was not staged.

search_log.query: (rapamycin OR sirolimus OR everolimus) AND aging
search_log.summary: Relevant no-results registries included NCT06727305, NCT07191353, and NCT05237687. NCT06727305 has SASP secondary endpoints but was posted before the delta window and remains recruiting with no results; the others are rapalog or functional-aging watch context without posted results.

search_log.query: ((senomorphic[Title/Abstract] OR senomorphics[Title/Abstract] OR "senescence-associated secretory phenotype"[Title/Abstract] OR SASP[Title/Abstract]) AND (aging[Title/Abstract] OR ageing[Title/Abstract] OR older[Title/Abstract] OR senescence[Title/Abstract])), publication dates 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned 41 records. The material primary result was PMID 42251040. Close non-staged records included PMID 42210884, PMID 42231423, PMID 42225652, PMID 41671944, and PMID 42224998.

search_log.query: mianserin[Title/Abstract] AND (senescence[Title/Abstract] OR aging[Title/Abstract] OR ageing[Title/Abstract] OR lifespan[Title/Abstract]), publication dates 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned one record, PMID 42251040, which was staged for review.

search_log.query: rapamycin/sirolimus/everolimus/mTOR with SASP and aging terms; metformin with SASP/senescence and aging terms; JAK/ruxolitinib/baricitinib with SASP/senescence and aging terms; publication dates 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Rapalog-specific search returned one review record, PMID 42225652. Metformin-specific search returned PMID 41671944, a computational phytochemical paper rather than metformin evidence. JAK-specific search returned PMID 42224998, an ABT-263/senescent-fibroblast cell paper rather than aging-directed JAK evidence. None changed the public outlook.

search_log.query: GLP-1/semaglutide/tirzepatide with senescence, skin, aging, or ageing terms; publication dates 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned 18 records, largely Dermatologic Surgery GLP-1 aesthetic or skin-context papers. PMID 42210884 was the closest senescence-marker item but was only a 3-person observational pilot, so it was not staged as an outlook-changing source.

trial_watch_checks.summary: The registry remains completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section; the peer-reviewed PMID 31761958 result remains the public evidence source. Staged study metadata adds checked and completion dates.

The parent registry is active, not recruiting, with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section and estimated November 2026 completion. The published older-adult bed-rest substudy remains a completed evidence item, so the staged update refreshes registry context without changing the evidence claim.

excluded_sources.title: Reduced Epidermal p21 Expression Is Identified in Some Subjects on Systemic GLP-1 Receptor Agonists: A Hypothesis-Generating Pilot Study of Metabolic Modulation in Human Skin Senescence.
excluded_sources.reason: Post-baseline human skin p21 pilot, but only three male adults were studied, exposure was observational rather than randomized, and no functional or clinical outcome was shown.

excluded_sources.title: Suppression of senescent metabolism of adipose tissue by rebalancing mitochondrial homeostasis via a selective drug delivery system.
excluded_sources.reason: Aged-mouse adipose delivery platform for dasatinib plus quercetin. It is closer to senolytics or delivery-platform surveillance than a clean senomorphics update.

excluded_sources.title: Insights into the therapeutic strategies for aging and aging-associated diseases.
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level aging therapy context. It does not add a new primary senomorphic result.

excluded_sources.title: Computational exploration of squalene analog 4,4'diapophytofluene as a potential anti-aging phytotherapeutic.
excluded_sources.reason: Computational phytochemical paper returned by the metformin/SASP search terms but not a metformin, older-adult, or senomorphics intervention result.

excluded_sources.title: IL-4/IL-13 signaling suppresses ABT-263-induced apoptosis in senescent human fibroblasts.
excluded_sources.reason: Senescent-fibroblast and ABT-263 cell-biology result, not an aging-directed JAK/senomorphics update.

excluded_sources.title: MTOR Inhibitors in Older Adults
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting mTOR inhibitor older-adult registry with SASP secondary endpoints and no posted results. The registry activity predates the June 1 baseline and did not produce a same-window evidence result.

excluded_sources.title: Resistance Training and Rapamycin to Enhance Bone Formation in Postmenopausal Women
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting everolimus plus resistance-training trial with no posted results and bone/muscle outcomes; useful watch context but not a same-window senomorphics result.

excluded_sources.title: The Role of Sirolimus in Preventing Functional Decline in Older Adults
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting sirolimus older-adult registry with no posted results, already better assigned to rapalogs/nutrient-sensing surveillance unless senescence/SASP results are posted.

excluded_sources.title: The Efficacy and Safety of Metformin Intervention in Elderly Overweight or Obesity With Mild Cognitive Impairment
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting metformin MCI/overweight older-adult registry with no results and no inspected senescence/SASP endpoint.

notes: The delta window is anchored to publication event publish-senomorphics-bootstrap-2026-06-01-2026-06-01t16-26-47-676z.
The staged outlook intentionally keeps evidence_stage, momentum, and confidence unchanged.

NCT03107884 is represented as a completed public evidence study because PMID 37486024 is already in the evidence layer; the parent registry status is kept in trial_details and should be rechecked after the estimated 2026-11-01 completion.
PubMed returned one future-dated GLP-1 muscle-repair record in the GLP-1 broad screen; it was outside the June 7 review date and was not considered.
next_actions: Review PMID 42251040 for source fidelity, especially the exact lifespan, healthspan, SASP, and human-cell claims.
Review the interpretation boundary that the new mianserin signal should not upgrade the human stage.
After review, publish the staged update if no blocking source or interpretation findings remain.
